Question: What is the purpose of a TRV withstand test on a circuit breaker?
Option_A: To verify the dielectric strength of the breaker
Option_B: To ensure the thermal stability of the breaker
Option_C: To check for mechanical endurance of the breaker
Option_D: To validate the timing accuracy of the breaker
Correct_Answer: To verify the dielectric strength of the breaker

Question: Which component has the most influence on TRV in a transmission network?
What parameter is critical when selecting a breaker for high RRRV conditions?
What does a successful TRV withstand test indicate?
Option_A: That the breaker can interrupt fault current safely
Option_B: That the breaker can withstand over-voltages
Option_C: That the breaker will not re-ignite
Option_D: That the breaker will trip instantly
Correct_Answer: That the breaker can interrupt fault current safely

Question: Which protection method helps reduce TRV impact on breakers?

Correct_Answer: Rate of Rise of Recovery Voltage (RRRV)
